rationale: In the conceptual development section, the citing paper explicitly uses Martin and Stewart (2001) to support its mechanism that similarity in brand identity cues can trigger transfer of associations. This mechanism is then used to motivate H1b, the prediction that imitation increases preference for the imitating private label relative to the imitated brand. Although cited in a grouped citation, it serves a functional role in hypothesis justification rather than a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's brand-equity transfer logic from brand-extension research to a private-label imitation context. It uses that prior evidence on similarity-driven association transfer to build its preference-shift hypothesis and conceptual framework.
